title: ES Core
text:
U.S. large-cap equity futures as a permanent core with dynamic allocation to eight alternatives across equities, fixed income, real assets, and digital assets.
← Back

title: ES Core
text:
Instrument Universe
The strategy trades seven futures contracts: ES (S&P 500), NQ (Nasdaq-100), RTY (Russell 2000), GC (Gold), SI (Silver), ZN (10-year Treasury), and MBT (Micro Bitcoin). All are USD-denominated and trade on regulated U.S. exchanges.
ES is the permanent core. U.S. large-cap equity has been the most reliably positive long-run risk premium available to investors. Every alternative is evaluated against ES on a risk-adjusted basis. The permanent equity core ensures the strategy participates in the growth regime that historically accounts for the majority of compounded long-run return.

title: ES Core
text:
Strategy Design
ES Core maintains a permanent allocation to ES and dynamically allocates up to 50% of the portfolio to the six alternative instruments based on their risk-adjusted performance relative to ES.
Framework:

Permanent core: ES with hard floor at 50%
Tactical sleeve: inverse downside semi-deviation weighted
Sleeve qualification: rolling Sortino vs ES > 0
Volatility target: 10–28% downside semi-deviation
Leverage: dynamic, max 3.0x
EMA(10) smoothing + 10% no-trade band
Signal lag: 1 day

Leverage is structurally necessary. The volatility targeting mechanism determines how much notional exposure each dollar of capital should support. In high-conviction, low-volatility environments the strategy runs above 1x notional leverage. In uncertain or high-volatility environments it contracts toward or below 1x.

title: Dog Capital
text: Quantitative Macro Strategies

title: Dog Capital
text:
About
Dog Capital is an independent quantitative research initiative focused on systematic volatility-targeting strategies. We build rules-based investment programs grounded in a single core thesis: the S&P 500 is the best asset to trade, and volatility is the best tool to manage it.
All strategies share the same framework — downside volatility targeting, dynamic leverage, and signal-lagged execution. Each program expresses that framework across a different market, capital size, and investor type.
Programs are developed entirely in-house. Backtest evidence and live performance are reported without adjustment.

title: Dog Capital
text:
Programs

ES Core
U.S. large-cap equity futures (ES) as a permanent core with dynamic allocation to seven alternatives across equities, fixed income, real assets, and digital assets. Downside volatility targeting in the 10–28% range. Dynamic leverage up to 3×. Daily rebalancing.
Backtest (Sep 2001–2026): CAGR 10.08% · Sharpe 0.515 · Max DD -48.26%
Strategy document →


SPY Core
Monthly rebalancing across four CEDEAR instruments (SPY, QQQ, GLD, IBIT) using the same vol targeting framework. S&P 500 as permanent core with a hard floor. No leverage. Designed for Argentine investors seeking USD-denominated returns with capital gains tax efficiency.
Backtest (Sep 2001–2026): CAGR 8.79% · Sharpe 0.677 · Max DD -33.29%
Strategy document →

title: Dog Capital
text:
Research
The conceptual foundation underlying each program is documented in the strategy papers linked above. The shared framework covers volatility estimation, signal construction, portfolio construction, and execution.
View research framework →

title: SPY Core
text:
Monthly rebalancing across four CEDEAR instruments using the same vol targeting framework as ES Core. S&P 500 as permanent core. No leverage.
← Back

title: SPY Core
text:
Instrument Universe
The strategy trades four instruments via CEDEARs: SPY (S&P 500), QQQ (Nasdaq-100), GLD (Gold), and IBIT (Bitcoin ETF).
SPY is the permanent core. The same organizing principle as ES Core applies: U.S. large-cap equity is the benchmark against which every alternative is evaluated on a risk-adjusted basis.
The strategy is designed for Argentine investors seeking USD-denominated returns. CEDEARs provide access to these instruments in the local market.

title: SPY Core
text:
Strategy Design
SPY Core maintains a permanent allocation to SPY and dynamically allocates the tactical sleeve to QQQ, GLD, and IBIT based on their risk-adjusted performance relative to SPY.
Framework:

Permanent core: SPY with hard floor at 50%
Tactical sleeve: inverse downside semi-deviation weighted
Sleeve qualification: rolling Sortino vs SPY > 0
Volatility target: 10–20% downside semi-deviation
Leverage: none (L_MAX = 1.0)
EMA(10) smoothing + 10% no-trade band
Signal lag: 1 day
Cadence: weekly signal, monthly execution
Idle capital: money market fund

DCA Scenario — $200/month
What a real investor accumulates starting with $10,000 and adding $200 every month, running the same monthly vol-targeting strategy throughout.



Strategy vs $200/month DCA


Grey dashed: SPY Buy & Hold + $200/month. Blue: SPY Core strategy + $200/month. Same capital deployed to both — the gap is the strategy’s alpha.
